content: For controllable canvas games, hold movement keys and assert that an exposed player position field or canvas pixels change because of input, not ambient animation.
For board or card games, test that selecting a legal object creates visible selection state, then committing a legal move changes board/current-player state.
For action/shooter games, press the primary action key and assert that projectile/action state or visible pixels change.
For every game, trigger restart and assert score, timers, game-over flags, pending effects, and active entities return to a clean initial state.
Verified session showed that controllable games need an input-delta check: hold movement/action keys and require state or canvas change attributable to input.
For point-and-click adventures, test a real puzzle chain: pick up an item, select it in inventory, use it on a matching hotspot, and expect scene/dialog/inventory state to change.

Expose the game's mutable state on window (window.state or window.game) so acceptance probes can read real fields. State kept only in closure scope is unprobeable and self-probes will read undefined and trivially fail.

recipe.err: game state is not exposed on window.state/window.game — probes cannot read real fields

content: For games that keep a score, hold the score as a numeric field on state (state.score) and reflect it in a visible HUD; the field should update on a scoring event rather than only living in a DOM string.

recipe.err: no numeric state.score exposed for the HUD

content: For timed games, drive the timer from delta-time in the update loop and expose it on state; the value should change over time while not paused, not be a static label set once.

recipe.err: no numeric time/timer field exposed on state

content: Implement pause as a state flag checked at the top of update() so entity motion freezes while paused and resumes cleanly; expose the flag (state.paused) so it can be verified.

recipe.err: no state.paused flag exposed — pause cannot be verified

content: Offer at least one discoverable control surface: keyboard handlers or on-screen touch buttons. Mobile-targeted goals must add visible touch controls so the game is playable without a keyboard.

recipe.err: no on-screen control element found (advisory; fine for keyboard-only desktop games)

content: Ensure the canvas exists with non-zero backing-store dimensions after load (set width/height for DPR) so the render target is real and not a zero-size element that silently draws nothing.

recipe.err: canvas missing or has zero width/height
